30.reason: Error: The null terminator should be placed at index i, not i+1, because the loop increments i after reading the last valid character, so i is the index of the non-letter character or one past the last letter. This causes the string to be incorrectly terminated, potentially including garbage or missing the last character.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The null terminator should be placed at index i, not i+1, because the loop increments i after reading the last valid character, so i is the index of the non-letter character or one past the last letter. This causes the string to be incorrectly terminated, potentially including garbage or missing the last character.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The null terminator should be placed at index i, not i+1, because the last valid character is at index i-1 after the loop increments i. This causes the string to be incorrectly terminated, potentially including garbage characters.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Null terminator placed after the last read character (which might be a non-letter). Should overwrite the non-letter with null terminator.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: Null-terminate at i, not i+1; i is index of non-letter character; should be word_tem[i]='\0'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incorrect null-termination index; should be word_tem[i] = '\0' to properly terminate the string at the non-letter character.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Should be word_tem[i]='\0'; because the loop exits when word_tem[i] is non-letter, so the valid letters are from 0 to i-1, and null terminator should be at index i.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Off-by-one error: after reading non-letter character at position i, the null terminator should be at word_tem[i], not i+1, because the loop exits when word_tem[i] is not a letter.]

49.reason: Error: The first argument to qsort should be the base address of the array, i.e., library, not library[0].ch. Passing library[0].ch incorrectly treats the first element's character array as the start of the array, leading to undefined behavior and incorrect sorting.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The first argument to qsort should be the base address of the array, i.e., library, not library[0].ch. Passing library[0].ch incorrectly treats the first element's character array as the start of the array, leading to undefined behavior and incorrect sorting.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The first argument to qsort should be the array to sort, i.e., library, not library[0].ch. Passing library[0].ch sorts only the first word's character array, not the entire array of structs.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  [First argument should be pointer to array of struct, not a char array. Also missing #include <stdlib.h> for qsort. Comparator is also incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Error: First argument should be library (the array), not library[0].ch; also missing #include <stdlib.h> for qsort.

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect first argument to qsort; should be library (the array), not library[0].ch (a char array inside the first element). Also, the comparison function is broken as noted earlier.]
